title: espeletia grandiflora subsp. subnivalis
intro: Espeletia grandiflora subsp. subnivalis, also known as the subnival espeletia or Andean giant, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to the Andes mountain range. It is believed to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are thought to contribute to its traditional use in treating various health issues, such as respiratory problems and digestive issues. The plant is said to have health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu, due to its ability to stimulate the immune system and promote wound healing. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
